titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/291

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=730 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.201135+00 | translation moa=731 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.184839+00

p.cl: Sciant omnes presentes et futuri quod

concessi et hac mea carta confirmaui deo et ecclesie beate
Marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus totam terram per suas rectas diuisas . quam Adam filius Ade de Dunelmo uen
didit eisdem monachis in territorio de Westlilliscliue . tenendam et habendam in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam cum omnibus
libertatibus et communibus asiamentis dicte uille de Weslill’ . adeo libere . quiete . plene et pacifice sicut carta quam habent de
dicto Adam testatur . Ego uero et heredes mei totam dictam terram cum omnibus suis pertinenciis dictis monachis contra omnes in
perpetuum warentizabimus ac defendemus .

In cuius rei testimonium ego pro me et heredibus meis presenti scripto sigillum
meum apponi feci .
Testibus . Willelmo filio Alex’ de Westlill’ . Roberto de Banget’ . Willelmo de Bernardeby . et aliis .
Let all people present and future know that

have granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the thowl land by its correct boundaries which Adam son of Adam of Durham sold to the same monks in the territory of West Lilliesleaf, to hold and to have in free and everlasting alms with all liberties and common easements of the said toun of West Lilliesleaf as freely, quitely, fully and peaceably as the charter which they have from the said Adam bears witness. I and my heirs shall warrant and defend the said land with all its pertinents for the said monks against all people for ever.
In testimony of this matter I for myself and my heirs have caused my seal to be applied to the present writing.
As witnesses: William son of Alexander of West Lilliesleaf; Robert of Bathgate; William of Bernoldby; and others .
